Djokovic has won 101 ATP Tour–level singles titles, including a record 24 majors (including a record ten Australian Open titles), a record 40 Masters, a record seven year-end championships, and an Olympic gold medal.
At Wimbledon, he reached the fourth round before losing to Mario Ančić in five sets.
Five weeks later, he beat Kevin Anderson in the fourth round in five sets, and then went on to claim his third Wimbledon title with a win over Roger Federer.
2023: Record-breaking 24th major & 7th ATP Finals titles
At the US Open, he triumphed over Medvedev in straight sets to win his fourth US Open title and a record-extending 24th men's singles major title overall.
2025: 100th ATP title and further records broken
At the inaugural Hellenic Championship, Djokovic defeated Lorenzo Musetti in the final to claim his 101st career ATP Tour-level title and his 72nd title won on hard courts, surpassing an all-time record he jointly held with Roger Federer.
His foundation has built 50 schools as of April 2022 and are building their 51st, and supported more than 20,800 children and over a thousand families.
Djokovic has won a record 72 Big Titles, including an all-time record of 24 Grand Slam titles, and holds the most weeks at No.

Since making his Wimbledon debut in 2005, Djokovic has tallied a 102-13 win-loss record at the tournament, including seven titles. ### What is Djokovic's best result at Wimbledon? Djokovic won Wimbledon in 2011, 2014, 2015, 2018, 2019, 2021 and 2022. The former No. 1 player in the PIF ATP Rankings beat Rafael Nadal in the final in 2011, Roger Federer in 2014, 2015 and 2019, Kevin Anderson in 2018, Matteo Berrettini in 2021 and Nick Kyrgios in 2022.
